codeReview.summary: Two targeted, minimal fixes to `.github/workflows/ci.yml`: (1) Changed grep pattern from `make \w+` to `make \K\w+` in the AGENTS.md validation step so only the target name is extracted, eliminating the shell word-splitting bug and the unnecessary awk call. (2) Removed the entire `reviewdog/action-govet@v1` step which referenced a non-existent GitHub Action; `golangci-lint` already runs `govet` as a default linter. Both changes are correct, minimal, and well-verified.

issues.description: The MAKE_TARGETS variable on line 293 is assigned but never used — it was left over from a prior iteration. The for loop on line 295 re-runs the grep independently. This is dead code but harmless and pre-existing (not introduced by this commit). Could be cleaned up in a future pass.

summary: PASS — Both fixes are correct and minimal. The diff shows exactly 2 insertions and 10 deletions in a single file. Verification confirmed: (1) `action-govet` no longer present, (2) `action-golangci-lint@v2` preserved, (3) `make \K\w+` grep pattern present, (4) tech debt check step remains intact immediately after the removed govet step, (5) AGENTS.md validation script correctly parses make targets. The worker followed the go-backend-worker skill procedure appropriately for a CI workflow fix (no Go test changes needed). One minor non-blocking note: the MAKE_TARGETS variable on line 293 is dead code but pre-existing.
